Off Calendar Mar-30-2017 Continued To Aug-21-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-18-2017 Continued To Jun-20-17 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Carolyn Doran's motion for summary adjudication of the third, fourth, thirteenth, fourteenth, twenty-fifth, and thirty-sixth affirmative defenses asserted in defendants ConforMIS, Inc. and Mark von Treskow's amended answer is continued to June 20, 2017, to be heard by Judge Murphy at 2:00 pm, in Department 302. The parties shall file revised separate statements that comply with CRC 3.1350. Plaintiff's revised separate statement shall be filed and served by no later than May 26, 2017. Defendants' separate statement in response shall be filed and served by no later than June 5, 2017. All courtesy copies must be delivered and lodged with Department 302 on the same date of filing. Per CRC 3.1350(d)(1)(B), Plaintiff's separate statement must separately identify each supporting material fact claimed to be without dispute with respect to the affirmative defense that is the subject of the motion. The separate statement should include only material facts and not any facts that are not pertinent to the disposition of the motion. (CRC 3.1350(d)(2).) Citation to the evidence in support of each material fact must include reference to the exhibit, title, page, and line numbers. (CRC 3.1350(d)(3).) The separate statement should have sections for each affirmative defense, and the evidence cited must support the fact stated. Plaintiff's current separate statement fails to comply with these rules. It includes facts not supported by the cited evidence (see UMF 59 and 74) and many times fails to include reference to page and line numbers in citations to evidence. Defendants' additional material facts stated in its reply separate statement must comply with CRC 3.1350(f)(3). Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR # 13358 =(302/HK)
